title: Save for a Goal
summary: Set a savings target and figure out how much to set aside.
learnerPromise: Set a savings target and figure out how much to set aside.
whyItMatters: Saving is not about having a lot of money. It is about having control. When you can save for a goal, you prove to yourself that you can delay gratification and make your future better.
realLifeUses: Save $300 for a new phone in 3 months instead of buying on credit.
Build an emergency fund so a flat tire does not become a crisis.
Put aside money for a trip, a course, or starting a small business.
Save for holiday gifts so you are not broke in January.
youWillLearn: Set a specific, measurable savings goal with a deadline.
Calculate how much to save per week or month.
Automate your savings so you do not have to think about it.
Track progress and adjust if you fall behind or get ahead.
miniChallenge: Pick one thing you want to save for and calculate how much to save per week.
steps: Pick one specific goal. Write down the exact amount you need.
Set a deadline. Divide the total by the number of weeks until then.
Open a dedicated savings account or envelope for this goal.
Set up an automatic transfer for that weekly amount — or do it manually every payday.
Check your progress every week. Celebrate milestones along the way.
completionCriteria: You have a written savings goal with a target amount and deadline.
You are saving a fixed amount weekly and can show your progress toward the goal.
commonProblems: The weekly amount feels too high — extend your deadline or reduce the goal amount.
You are tempted to spend the saved money — keep it in a separate account you cannot easily access.
Life happens and you miss a week — do not quit. Resume the next week and adjust if needed.
tips: Name your savings account after your goal — 'New Laptop Fund' is harder to spend from.
Automate transfers on payday so you never see the money in your spending account.
Visual trackers help — draw a progress bar and fill it in as you save.
